---
title: "Glossary"
---

<!--
GENERATED FILE. DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY.

Edit instead:
- data/glossary.csv for glossary content
- data/bhagavadgita_ai_refined.xlsx for verses/translations
- tools/0_generate_site.py for generation logic
- styles.css for visual style

Then run:
    run_all.bat
-->


<div class="site-note glossary-note">
A compact guide to transliterated Sanskrit terms and recurring names used in the English rendering.
</div>

## Concepts

<div class="glossary-list">
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-atman">
<h3>Atman</h3>
<p>Self or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or personality.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Atman, Ātman</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-bhakti">
<h3>bhakti</h3>
<p>Devotion or devoted participation, especially toward the divine.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: bhakti, bhakti-yoga</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-brahman">
<h3>Brahman</h3>
<p>The ultimate reality or absolute principle.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Brahma-nirvana, Brahma-sūtra, Brahma-yoga, Brahman, Brahman-nirvana, Brahman-realization</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-buddhi">
<h3>buddhi</h3>
<p>Intellect, discernment, or decisive understanding.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: buddhi, buddhi-yoga</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-dharma">
<h3>dharma</h3>
<p>Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: dharma, dharma-field</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-guna">
<h3>guna</h3>
<p>A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: guna, gunas, guṇa, guṇas</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-karma">
<h3>karma</h3>
<p>Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: karma, karma-yoga</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-kshetra">
<h3>kshetra</h3>
<p>Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: kshetra, kṣetra</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-kshetrajna">
<h3>kshetrajna</h3>
<p>Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: kshetrajna, kṣetrajña</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-maya">
<h3>maya</h3>
<p>Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Maya, maya, māyā, Māyā, yoga-maya</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-nirvana">
<h3>nirvana</h3>
<p>Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Brahma-nirvana, Brahman-nirvana, nirvana, nirvāṇa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-prakriti">
<h3>prakriti</h3>
<p>Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: prakriti, Prakriti, prakṛti, Prakṛti</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-purusha">
<h3>purusha</h3>
<p>Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: purusha, Purusha, Purushottama, puruṣa, Puruṣa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-rajas">
<h3>rajas</h3>
<p>The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: rajas, Rajas, rājasika</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-samsara">
<h3>samsara</h3>
<p>The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: samsara, saṃsāra</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-sankhya">
<h3>Sankhya</h3>
<p>A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Sankhya, Sāṅkhya, Sāṅkhya-yoga, Sāṅkhyas</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-sannyasa">
<h3>sannyasa</h3>
<p>Renunciation or giving up, especially of possessive attachment to action.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: sannyasa, sannyāsa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-sattva">
<h3>sattva</h3>
<p>The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: sattva, Sattva, sāttvika</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-tamas">
<h3>tamas</h3>
<p>The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: tamas, Tamas, tāmasika</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-tyaga">
<h3>tyaga</h3>
<p>Abandonment, renunciation, or relinquishing attachment to the fruits of action.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: tyaga, tyāga</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-yoga">
<h3>yoga</h3>
<p>Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;).</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: bhakti-yoga, buddhi-yoga, karma-yoga, practice-yoga, Sāṅkhya-yoga, yoga, yoga-maya, yogi, yogī</p>
</section>
</div>

## Social and ritual terms

<div class="glossary-list">
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-acharya">
<h3>acharya</h3>
<p>Teacher or preceptor.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: acharya, ācārya</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-brahmin">
<h3>Brahmin</h3>
<p>A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: brahmin, Brahmin, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇas</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-kshatriya">
<h3>kshatriya</h3>
<p>Member of the warrior or ruling class.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: kshatriya, kṣatriya</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-om">
<h3>Om</h3>
<p>Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: AUM, Om</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-shudra">
<h3>shudra</h3>
<p>Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: shudra, shudras, śūdra, śūdras</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-vaishya">
<h3>vaishya</h3>
<p>Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: vaishya, vaishyas, vaiśya, vaiśyas</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-veda">
<h3>Vedas</h3>
<p>Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Bṛhat-sāma, Gāyatrī, Sāma, Veda, Vedas, Ṛg</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-yajna">
<h3>yajna</h3>
<p>Sacrifice, ritual offering, or consecrated action.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: adhiyajña, yajna, yajña</p>
</section>
</div>

## Names and epithets

<div class="glossary-list">
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-acyuta">
<h3>Acyuta</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Acyuta</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-arjuna">
<h3>Arjuna</h3>
<p>Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Arjuna</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-bharata">
<h3>Bharata</h3>
<p>A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Bharata, Bharatas, Bhārata, Bhāratas</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-dhananjaya">
<h3>Dhananjaya</h3>
<p>Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Dhananjaya, Dhanañjaya</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-govinda">
<h3>Govinda</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Govinda</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-gudakesha">
<h3>Gudakesha</h3>
<p>Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Gudakesha, Guḍākeśa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-hrishikesha">
<h3>Hrishikesha</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Hrishikesha, Hṛṣīkeśa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-janardana">
<h3>Janardana</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Janardana, Janārdana</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-kaunteya">
<h3>Kaunteya</h3>
<p>Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Kaunteya, Kunti</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-keshava">
<h3>Keshava</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Keshava, Keśava</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-krishna">
<h3>Krishna</h3>
<p>Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Krishna, Kṛṣṇa</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-madhava">
<h3>Madhava</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Madhava, Mādhava</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-madhusudana">
<h3>Madhusudana</h3>
<p>Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Madhusudana, Madhusūdana</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-pandava">
<h3>Pandava</h3>
<p>One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Pandava, Pandavas, Pāṇḍava, Pāṇḍavas, Pāṇḍu</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-partha">
<h3>Partha</h3>
<p>Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Partha, Pārtha, Pṛthā</p>
</section>
<section class="glossary-entry" id="glossary-vasudeva">
<h3>Vasudeva</h3>
<p>Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva.</p>
<p class="glossary-variants">Forms in the text: Vasudeva, Vāsudeva</p>
</section>
</div>
